data.title: Despite highest G-10 rates, LPL economist asserts Fed justified. Trump pressures Powell exit but independence crucial. Whit high rate graph.
data.description: Jeffrey Roach, Chief Economist at LPL Financial, notes that the U.S. Fed, despite having the highest policy rate among G-10 nations, does not need immediate aggressive rate cuts like its peers. The U.S. Federal Open Market Committee's Target Rate is notably above Japan, Canada, Euro Area, and the U.K., extending to mid-2025. Roach attributes this difference to the exceptional U.S. economy, deep capital markets, and strong legal structure. He emphasizes the importance of Fed independence in pursuing its congressional mandate of full employment and stable prices without outside influence. Roach's insights were shared in collaboration with Benzinga and LPL Financial.

data.title: Polymarket predicts 50% chance of Fed rate cut in Sep meeting, with 50% odds for 25 bp cut. CME FedWatch tracker shows 56% chance for rate cut.
data.description: Crypto gamblers are anticipating a 50% probability of the Federal Reserve implementing the first interest rate reduction of 2025 at the upcoming September gathering. On Polymarket, the chances of a 25 basis-point decrease stood at 50%, while the odds of rates remaining in the current 4.25%-4.50% range were at 43%. The likelihood of the central bank maintaining rates during the July session was at a high 96%. These predictions align closely with the CME FedWatch tracker, indicating a 56% likelihood of a 0.25% rate cut. Further details are accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Momo crowd behavior triggers yellow flag for stock market; GM loses $1.1B due to tariffs. OPEND stock volume hits 1.82B, 3 times float shares. Market caution advised due to aggressive pumping in media.
data.description: The news article discusses the aggressive surge in Opendoor Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ: OPEN) stock price due to media pumping, not fundamental developments. The stock saw a massive increase in volume, reaching 1.82 billion shares, significantly higher than the average volume of 137,000 shares. The float stands at 655.06 million shares, with trading volume exceeding the float by about three times. Afternoon trading saw a rapid drop in stock value, possibly due to a large seller selling off shares, as indicated by the VUD indicator measuring net supply and demand. The article notes that the momentum (momo) crowd's buying behavior is largely influenced by media pumps, and in the past week, this crowd has displayed more reckless behavior, prompting a cautionary alert.

data.title: Major cryptos rebound, Fed maintains rate, ATH nears. WH issues crypto report, US urged to lead. SEC sets standards for crypto ETPs. ETH turns 10, Robinhood's crypto revenue doubles. Coinbase, JPM connect banks to wallets. $57m ETH purchase, Fundamental Global to acquire $200m ETH. Hyperliquid offers refunds for downtime. CoinDCX employee arrested for $44m hack. NASDAQ hosts key ETH leaders.
data.description: Major cryptocurrencies have bounced back from recent losses as the White House's crypto report met lukewarm reception. Congressman Atkins emphasized the need for the US to take the lead in the crypto revolution. The SEC has provided guidelines for listing standards for crypto Exchange-Traded Products (ETPs). Ethereum celebrates its 10 year anniversary with prominent figures gathering at NASDAQ. Robinhood surpassed expectations with its crypto revenue doubling. Coinbase and JPMorgan Chase are set to integrate bank accounts with crypto wallets. An Ethereum machine recently purchased $57 million worth of ETH, while Fundamental Global plans to invest $200 million in ETH. Hyperliquid has announced refunds for users affected by downtime. Additionally, a CoinDCX employee has been arrested in connection to a $44 million hack.

data.title: CSC issues revised guidance on NIL payments for college athletes in class action settlement.
data.description: On July 31, 2025, in San Francisco, the College Sports Commission (CSC) released updated instructions regarding the necessity of a legitimate business rationale for compensating college athletes for names, images, and likenesses (NIL) in accordance with the terms outlined in the recent class action settlement.

data.title: Coface SA reports €937m turnover, 12.6% annualized return on equity. Annual net income down 12.7% at €124.2m. Expands services with acquisitions and technology division. Strengthening credit insurance business. Net loss ratio at 40.1%, combined ratio at 71.3%. Acquires Cedar Rose and Novertur International.%">Coface SA: Reports €937m turnover & 12.6% ROE, net income down 12.7% at €124.2m. Expands with acquisitions & tech division.
data.description: Coface has reported a strong start to the year, with an annualized return on tangible equity at 12.6% and a turnover of €937 million, showing an increase of +2.3% at constant FX and perimeter. Key highlights include a trade credit insurance revenue growth of +1.7%, client activity up +1.8%, and client retention at 94.0%. The net loss ratio was at 40.1%, up 5.1%, and the net combined ratio was at 71.3%, up 7.9%. Despite a net income decrease of 12.7% compared to H1-24, strategic investments in business information and debt collection saw growth. The estimated solvency ratio was reported at 195%, above the target range. Notable strategic investments include strengthening governance, the acquisition of Cedar Rose and Novertur International, and the creation of a Lloyd's syndicate. CEO Xavier Durand highlighted the global rise in bankruptcies and increased tariffs impacting the business environment.

data.title: Tile Shop Holdings, Inc. (Nasdaq: TTSH) will report Q2 financial results on August 7, 2025, with a webcast at 9:00 a.m. EST. Currently, they operate 140 stores in 31 states and D.C. #TTSH #Q2Results
data.description: Tile Shop Holdings, Inc. (Nasdaq: TTSH), a retailer of natural stone tiles, luxury vinyl tiles and related products, will release its Q2 2025 financial results on August 7, 2025, at 7:00 am Eastern Time. The accompanying webcast for investors will begin at 9:00 am Eastern Time on the same day, hosted by the CEO, CFO, and the Investor Relations representative. The company operates 140 stores across 31 states and D.C., offering quality products and customer service. Investors are advised to monitor the company's website for relevant information.

data.title: NV5 Global Inc. shareholders approve merger with Acuren Corp with majority in favor at Special Meeting.
data.description: In Hollywood, Florida on July 31, 2025, NV5 Global, Inc. (Nasdaq: NVEE), a prominent firm offering tech-enabled engineering, testing, inspection, and consulting services for the built environment, disclosed that the majority of its common stockholders have endorsed the planned merger with Acuren Corporation ("Acuren"). This approval was attained at a Special Meeting of Stockholders conducted on the same day.

data.title: Ferrari Q2 earnings beat estimates: Revenue $2.03B, $2.70 EPS, 4.4% YoY increase, 3,494 units shipped. Stock slides.
data.description: Luxury car manufacturer Ferrari N.V. disclosed its second-quarter financial results, surpassing analyst expectations. In Q2, the company generated revenue of $2.03 billion, beating estimates of $1.82 billion. Earnings stood at $2.70 per share, exceeding the projected $2.39 per share. The company observed a 4.4% year-over-year increase in total revenue, attributed to shipping 3,494 units in the quarter. For a comprehensive overview, the full report is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Howmet Aerospace reports record revenue of $2.05 billion in Q2 2025, with net income of $407 million and adjusted EPS of 91 cents, leading to an upward revision of its 2025 outlook.
data.description: Howmet Aerospace Inc. (NYSE: HWM) released its second-quarter 2025 financial results, reporting record revenue of $2.05 billion, up 9% from the previous year. The company saw growth in commercial aerospace (8%), defense aerospace (21%), and industrial and other markets (17%). Net income was $407 million, or $1.00 per share, with adjusted earnings per share up 36% to 91 cents. Adjusted EBITDA increased by 22% to $589 million, with margins expanding by 300 basis points. The company generated $344 million in free cash flow, with operating cash flow at $446 million and a cash balance of $546 million. Engine Products led segment performance with a 13% revenue increase to $1.1 billion. Analysts are eyeing further gains as Howmet Aerospace strengthens its position in the aerospace and defense sectors.

data.title: Tether's Q2 report revealed $13.4B in USDT added, surpassing $127B in US Treasuries. Net profit at $4.9B.
data.description: During the latest quarter, Tether (USDT) revealed in its Q2 2025 report an addition of over $13.4 billion in USDT, bringing the net new issuance for the year to over $20 billion, and the total USDT supply now exceeds $157 billion. The report indicates Tether's exposure to U.S. Treasuries surged to $127 billion, with $105.5 billion in direct holdings and $21.3 billion indirectly held, up from around $119 billion in Q1. This significant exposure ranks Tether among the top holders of U.S. sovereign debt globally, alongside institutional investors and central banks. In the second quarter, Tether recorded a net profit of approximately $4.9 billion, with $3.1 billion coming from recurring operational earnings and the rest around $1.8 billion.

data.title: Clearwater launches ECRM solution for healthcare cyber risk management. Largest provider in industry.
data.description: In Nashville, Tennessee on July 31, 2025, Clearwater, a leading provider of cybersecurity and compliance solutions for the healthcare sector, unveiled its latest product, the Enterprise Cyber Risk Management (ECRM) solution. This new offering is aimed at assisting healthcare organizations in managing their cybersecurity risks effectively.
